scope.summary: R3 adds a live-serve Chromium lane that records the pre-A5 browser pairing failure, then proves authenticated operator action POST 200 and EventSource Last-Event-ID resume against the real listener.
out_of_scope: A5 dashboard fetch-first/product fix remains open; this close does not accept Origin:null or change dashboard security policy.
No Oracle schema/live database assertions are claimed; the action proof uses server-answered oracle_capabilities to isolate the browser/operator HTTP surface.

entry_points.name: standalone R3 lane
entry_points.trace: bash scripts/rig/rig_browser_lane.sh run --log builds oraclemcp through omcpb, runs web/playwright.live-serve.config.ts, and writes target/e2e/browser-lane/<run>/browser-lane-result.json.

entry_points.name: Tier-3 rig wiring
entry_points.trace: bash scripts/rig/rig.sh browser-lane runs the new lane under Tier A pseudo-home; bash scripts/rig/rig.sh run now includes the browser lane after L1.

entry_points.name: latest live artifact
entry_points.trace: target/e2e/browser-lane/run-1784711617436-a69b4e82-a4d7-45a2-94a9-92c6e2ba4c00/browser-lane-result.json recorded expected_pre_a5_failure=true, browser_pairing.status=403, browser_pairing.origin=null, action_status=200, action_tool=oracle_capabilities, and sse_event_ids=[operator/1, operator/2].

entry_points.name: scoped core compile
entry_points.trace: env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo check -p oraclemcp-core passed: oraclemcp-db and oraclemcp-core checked successfully with the checkout target.

entry_points.name: browser lane syntax and discovery
entry_points.trace: bash -n scripts/rig/rig_browser_lane.sh and bash -n scripts/rig/rig.sh passed; npm --prefix web exec -- playwright test --config web/playwright.live-serve.config.ts --list passed and listed 1 Chromium live-serve browser test.

entry_points.name: R3 live browser lane
entry_points.trace: env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR scripts/swarm_discipline.sh bounded-run --timeout 600 -- bash scripts/rig/rig_browser_lane.sh run --log passed: omcpb built oraclemcp, Playwright ran 1 Chromium test against live serve, and the rig completed with pass.

limitations: The lane is intentionally in pre-A5 expected-failure mode by default: real Chromium form pairing currently records 403 from Origin:null. Set OMCP_BROWSER_LANE_EXPECT_PRE_A5_FAILURE=0 after A5 lands to enforce POST -> 303 -> /.
The authenticated action and SSE resume proof run after a direct same-origin pairing bootstrap installs the dashboard cookie, because the current browser form pairing is the known A5 blocker.
No full-workspace cargo gate is claimed from this shared dirty checkout.

known_defects.description: A5 must flip browser pairing from the recorded Origin:null 403 to the green POST -> 303 -> / path without accepting literal Origin:null.
